note_data.title: The biological approach
keyPoints: The biological approach views humans as biological organisms where all thoughts, ideas and cognitive processes must be biological in origin - the mind 'lives' within the brain
Heritability coefficients quantify the genetic basis of characteristics, with IQ having a coefficient of 0.5 according to Plomin, showing equal influence of nature and nurture
An individual's genotype is their genetic make-up, while phenotype is the physical expression of genotype - interaction between phenotype and environment results in behaviour
Two people may have the same genotype but different phenotypes due to personal choices or epigenetics - changes in gene expression without altering genetic make-up
Natural selection is the mechanism of evolution where genetically-determined behaviours giving selective advantage increase survival and reproduction chances
Examples of behaviours with selective advantage include avoiding fire and deep water (Seligman's prepared stimuli), longer necks for reaching food, and cows producing more milk
Understanding biological processes has led to development of psychoactive drugs like dopamine antagonists (Chlorpromazine) for schizophrenia treatment
Biological determinism suggests behaviour is caused by internal biological forces over which we have no control, raising concerns about judicial responsibility and economic discrimination

definitions.definition: The study of the influence of an organism's genetic composition on its behaviour and the interaction of heredity and environment insofar as they affect behaviour
An individual's genetic make-up, where a gene is a short section of DNA coding for specific proteins
The physical expression of an individual's genotype
A change in gene expression, without altering an individual's genetic make-up through epigenetic markers like DNA methylation
The mechanism of evolution where genetically-determined behaviour giving selective advantage increases chances of surviving, reproducing and passing beneficial alleles to offspring
The process by which organisms change over time as a result of changes in heritable physical or behavioural traits

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give basic details without detailed explanation. 2-3 sentences maximum.
Give detailed account including what, how, when, where. Use examples.

modelAnswer.question: Explain one assumption of the biological approach. [3 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: One assumption of the biological approach is that all behaviour can be explained in terms of bodily processes. This means that psychological phenomena such as mental disorders, personality traits and cognitive abilities are caused by biological factors such as brain structure, neurochemistry and genetics. For example, depression is explained by low levels of the neurotransmitter serotonin rather than psychological factors.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Identifies assumption - behaviour explained by bodily processes
Mark 2: Elaboration - mentions brain structure, neurochemistry, genetics
Mark 3: Example - depression and serotonin levels

commonMistakes: Confusing genotype and phenotype - remember genotype is genetic make-up while phenotype is physical expression
Assuming MZ twins' higher concordance rates are purely genetic without considering they may share more similar environments than DZ twins
Overlooking that two people can have identical genotypes but different phenotypes due to environmental factors or personal choices
Failing to distinguish between natural selection (the mechanism) and evolution (the overall process of change over time)
Not recognising that epigenetics involves changes in gene expression without changing the actual genetic make-up
examinerTips: When evaluating drug treatments, contrast their minimal patient effort requirements with time-consuming cognitive therapies like CBT
Discuss real-world implications of biological determinism including potential impacts on judicial systems and employment/insurance discrimination
Use specific examples like Chlorpromazine as dopamine antagonists and Plomin's IQ heritability coefficient of 0.5 to demonstrate detailed knowledge
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Brain diagram showing cerebral cortex (thinking/decision making), hippocampus (memory formation), amygdala (fear processing), and neuron diagram showing synapse with neurotransmitter release from presynaptic to postsynaptic neuron
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to explain how the biological approach would explain aggressive behavior, requiring discussion of genetics, brain structures, and hormones
This topic appeared in O/N 2022 P2 Q2 (4 marks) — students had to evaluate the biological approach using one strength and one weakness with psychological evidence
This topic was examined in M/J 2022 P1 Q15 (1 mark) — multiple choice question asking which brain area is associated with aggression according to biological approach

This topic was tested in O/N 2023 P2 Q3 (8 marks) — students had to compare biological and cognitive approaches to explaining mental health disorders, requiring detailed knowledge of both perspectives

workedExample.question: Explain how the biological approach would explain depression. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify the biological factors - mention genetics, neurotransmitters, and brain structure
Step 2: Explain genetic influence - depression runs in families, twin studies show 40% heritability
Step 3: Discuss neurotransmitter imbalance - low levels of serotonin and norepinephrine affect mood regulation
Step 4: Describe brain structure differences - hippocampus and prefrontal cortex abnormalities in depressed individuals
Step 5: Link to treatment - antidepressants work by increasing neurotransmitter availability, supporting biological explanation
workedExample.answer: The biological approach explains depression through genetic, neurochemical, and neuroanatomical factors. Genetic studies show depression has 40% heritability, with higher concordance rates in identical twins. Neurotransmitter imbalances, particularly low serotonin and norepinephrine, disrupt mood regulation and emotional processing. Brain imaging reveals structural differences including reduced hippocampus volume and decreased prefrontal cortex activity in depressed individuals. The effectiveness of antidepressant medications that increase neurotransmitter availability provides further evidence for biological causation.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for genetics, 1 mark for example/evidence, 1 mark for neurotransmitters, 1 mark for specific chemicals, 1 mark for brain structure, 1 mark for supporting evidence/treatment

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused correlation with causation when discussing biological factors' — always distinguish between biological influences and direct biological causes

Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often gave vague answers about 'brain differences' without specifying structures or functions' — always name specific brain areas and their roles when discussing neurological factors

Examiner Report 2023: 'Candidates frequently ignored the reductionist nature of biological explanations in evaluation questions' — remember to discuss how biological approach oversimplifies complex behaviors by ignoring environmental and psychological factors
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw a simple neuron diagram showing: cell body (soma), dendrites, axon, axon terminals, and synaptic gap. Include arrows showing direction of electrical impulse and label where neurotransmitters are released. This demonstrates understanding of how biological processes (neural transmission) underlie psychological phenomena. Alternative diagram: simple brain cross-section labeling key structures (frontal lobe, limbic system, brain stem) relevant to behavior and mental processes.
note_data.quickRecap: The biological approach assumes all behaviour originates from biological processes including genes, hormones and neurotransmitters. Genotype (genetic make-up) expresses as phenotype (physical traits), with behaviour resulting from phenotype-environment interaction. Natural selection favours genetically-determined behaviours with selective advantage. The approach has practical applications in drug development but raises concerns about biological determinism and its social implications.
